name: Gualba, Riells, Santa Fe, Sant Celoni - circular - Parque Natural de Montseny
region_intro: Mountain biking around Gualba offers diverse terrain within the Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The region is characterized by rugged topography, dense forests of cork oak and holm oak, and the Riera de Gualba stream with its impressive Salt de Gualba waterfall. This varied landscape provides a mix of challenging mountain trails and more accessible paths, catering to different skill levels. The area's natural features, including its streams and wooded valleys, create engaging routes for mountain bike enthusiasts. Best mountain bike trails around Gualba The most popular mountain bike trail is Gualba, Riells, Santa Fe, Sant Celoni - circular - Parque Natural de Montseny, a difficult 36.3 miles (58.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through the Montseny Natural Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sant Martí de Montnegre Church – Can Benet Pass loop from Estació de Gualba, a moderate 22.6 miles (36.4 km) path. This loop offers varied terrain with views of the surrounding natural park. Local mountain bikers also love the Camí Real Park – Lateral A-P7 loop from Estació de Gualba, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail leading through the Camí Real Park, often completed in about 1 hour 12 minutes. Mountain biking around Gualba is defined by dense forests, rugged topography, and the Riera de Gualba stream. The network offers more than 170 routes with options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Gualba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 380 reviews. More than 3100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Gualba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Gualba?
A: Gualba offers a wide variety of mountain bike trails, with over 170 routes available. These routes cater to different skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Gualba?
A: The terrain around Gualba is characterized by its rugged topography, dense forests of cork oak and holm oak, and the presence of the Riera de Gualba stream. You'll find a mix of challenging mountain trails and more accessible paths, all set within the diverse landscapes of the Montseny Natural Park.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Gualba?
A: Yes, Gualba offers several easier routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31522863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí Real Park – Lateral A-P7 loop from Estació de Gualba&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 10.6-mile (17.1 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 12 minutes, providing a good option for a less strenuous ride. The Parc Mediambiental de Gualba also features marked itineraries that are generally more accessible.

Q: Where can I find routes with significant elevation gain for a challenging ride?
A: For a challenging ride with significant elevation, consider routes that venture deeper into the Montseny Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968718794/gualba-riells-santa-fe-sant-celoni-circular-parque-natural-de-montseny" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gualba, Riells, Santa Fe, Sant Celoni - circular - Parque Natural de Montseny&lt;/a&gt; trail is a difficult 36.3-mile (58.5 km) route with nearly 1,500 meters of elevation gain, offering a substantial workout.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Gualba?
A: Gualba is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the Riera de Gualba stream, known for its foamy cascades, and visit viewpoints for the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4552641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Gualba&lt;/a&gt; waterfall. The trails also wind through diverse forests and offer views of the Montseny massif. Notable peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/226426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matagalls (1697 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1687529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Home (Man's Hill)&lt;/a&gt; are visible from various points in the park.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Gualba?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Gualba are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5334502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Martí de Montnegre Church – Can Benet Pass loop from Estació de Gualba&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 22.6-mile (36.4 km) circular path that offers varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Gualba?
A: The mountain bike trails in Gualba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 380 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful natural park setting, and the variety of routes available for all skill levels.

Q: Can I find any historical landmarks or castles along the mountain bike routes?
A: While the primary focus is natural landscapes, you can find historical points of interest nearby. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6961969" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Breda – Cloister of Sant Salvador loop from Gualba&lt;/a&gt; takes you past cultural sites. Additionally, the region features several castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2092377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montsoriu Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3309501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostalric Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored as separate attractions.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails that offer a good balance of challenge and scenery?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38255027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hortsavinya Lime Kiln – Can Benet Pass loop from Gualba&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 39.7-kilometer (24.7 miles) route that provides a good balance. It features over 790 meters of elevation gain and takes you through scenic parts of the Montseny Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Gualba?
A: Gualba's diverse forests and varied terrain make it enjoyable across seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While winter mountain biking is possible, higher elevations in Montseny Natural Park may experience colder conditions. Summer can be warm, so early morning rides are often preferred.

Q: Are there any amenities like picnic areas or cafes near the trails?
A: Yes, the Parc Mediambiental de Gualba, which covers over 60 hectares of forest, offers amenities such as picnic and barbecue areas, making it a convenient spot for a break during or after your ride. You'll also find cafes and restaurants in the town of Gualba and surrounding villages.
tips: The first documentary record of the site of "Riellos" is found in a precept dated September 9, 878. With the founding of the monastery of Breda in 1038, it came under its direct jurisdiction. In 1169 there is already written evidence that the church of Riells is dedicated to Sant Martí de Tours. In 1957 the parish became part of the Bishopric of Barcelona and the Bishopric of Girona. Sant Martí de Riells is currently the parish church of Riells i Viabrea.
The Col de Té is where the restaurant of the same name is located. It is a good place to rest or take a photo of the beautiful views.
Hotel Santa Fe was built in the early 20th century. It is surrounded by big beautiful trees and has many nice hiking trails.
Accessible by mountain bike, walking. By car too, but then you have to walk.
The Mirador de les Goitadores is a viewpoint of the Province of Barcelona located in the Montseny massif, near Can Pinyoca and Turó de Castellar.
A small and pleasant lake next to the trail and near the Mirador de les Goitadores.
